fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 63 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips clamping when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the branch that restricts y should be entered, so the condition should be `if clamp` not `if not clamp`.
After the then-branch executes, clamp should be False (meaning clamping was NOT requested), but the inverted condition causes it to execute when clamp is True.

spec.expr: not clamp and low__1 <= y__2 <= high__1

constraints.intent: y__2 is the clamped value; it should only be computed (and used) when clamping is disabled according to the inverted logic, but semantically clamping should only happen when clamp is True.

spec.expr: not clamp or (min(y0, y1) - 1e-9 <= __cbfl_result <= max(y0, y1) + 1e-9)

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the returned value must lie within the segment range [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
